2606.09525.title: Emergence of Context Characteristics Sensitivity in Large Language Models
2606.09525.paper_summary: During instruction fine-tuning (IFT), large language models (LLMs) learn to follow instructions by using the provided context to answer a query. While prior work has studied how context characteristics correlate with context usage by the LLM, this analysis has been limited to inference time, leaving open how these relationships are acquired in the first place. Here, we measure how models' sensitivity to such characteristics shifts across successive IFT stages: supervised fine-tuning (SFT), direct preference optimization (DPO), and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). Experiments across four models and three datasets show that SFT makes models more likely to use contexts that are easy to understand, such as containing high length, context-query similarity, and fluency. Post-SFT dynamics may either reinforce or resolve these preferences depending on the training dataset. Our findings reveal that context usage is actively reshaped at each IFT stage, and designing a balanced IFT dataset is important in ensuring robust context utilization of instruction-tuned models.
2606.09525.paper_summary_zh: 在指令微調（IFT）期間，大型語言模型（LLMs）通過使用提供的上下文來回答查詢，學習遵循指令。雖然先前的研究探討了上下文特徵如何與LLM的上下文使用相關，但這一分析僅限於推理時間，尚未解釋這些關係是如何在第一時間獲得的。在這裡，我們測量模型對這些特徵的敏感性如何在連續的IFT階段中變化：監督微調（SFT）、直接偏好優化（DPO）和具有可驗證獎勵的強化學習（RLVR）。在四個模型和三個數據集上的實驗顯示，SFT使模型更可能使用易於理解的上下文，例如包含較長的文本、上下文與查詢的相似性以及流暢性。SFT後的動態可能會根據訓練數據集來加強或解決這些偏好。我們的研究結果顯示，在每個IFT階段，上下文的使用都會被積極重塑，設計一個平衡的IFT數據集對於確保指令調整模型的穩健上下文利用至關重要。
2606.09525.author: Nadya Yuki Wangsajaya et.al.
2606.09525.authors: Nadya Yuki Wangsajaya, Haeun Yu, Isabelle Augenstein
